title: Angorkis saccifera
taxonomy: Angorkis saccifera is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Araliaceae. It is native to the tropical regions of the Pacific Islands. The exact classification of Angorkis saccifera is as follows: it belongs to the genus Angorkis, within the family Araliaceae, and the order Apiales. The plant's classification is further detailed as being in the kingdom Plantae, and the phylum Magnoliophyta. Its classification plays a significant role in understanding its evolutionary history and relationships with other plants.

The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its various health benefits, which include reducing inflammation and improving digestion. intro: Angokis saccifera, also known as the Anisacanthus thurberi or Desert Marigold, is a flowering plant native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. The plant is rich in health benefits, including its ability to reduce inflammation, improve digestion, and exhibit antioxidant properties, making it a potential natural remedy for various ailments. The bioactive constituents of Angokis saccifera include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The plant is commonly used in herbal preparations such as teas, tinctures, and capsules, often in combination with other herbs, to support immune function and overall well-being.
